description: Setting a long queue-wait/batch window that maximizes throughput but pushes p99 latency past the SLO.
body: Failure mode: the batch timeout is tuned for maximum throughput, so requests wait a long time to fill batches and tail latency balloons even though average latency and throughput look great. Detection signals: p99/p99.9 far exceeds the SLO while mean latency is fine; latency improves immediately when the queue wait is lowered; benchmarks only reported the mean. Do not tune batching on throughput alone. Safer fallback: measure p99 under load, cap the queue wait, and choose the largest batch that keeps the tail under the SLO.
